what_it_does: Styles a single composer input surface with a shared base layout and three context-specific variants (hero, floating bar, inline), plus action buttons, a leading mark, and starting-point chips.
observations: Uses a BEM-like naming scheme: .composer block, .composer__* elements, and .composer--* modifiers.
Base .composer is a flex row with a pill-shaped background, subtle inset highlight, soft shadow, and a focus-within accent ring using var(--accent-gold).
.composer__input is a transparent, borderless, outlineless flex child that takes remaining space.
.composer__actions holds circular buttons; .composer__btn--send has a gold gradient, shadow, and disabled state.
.composer--bar is position:fixed, centered with margin-inline:auto rather than a transform, and includes a comment explaining this is to keep transform free for a shared-element FLIP animation.
.composer--hero, .composer--bar, and .composer--inline only change padding, gaps, font sizes, and button dimensions.
Includes a .composer__chips block under the hero variant with small pill-shaped starting-point suggestions.
Uses CSS custom properties with inline fallback values in some gradient declarations.
Applies backdrop-filter: blur(14px) only on the bar variant.
The file header states it is loaded after legacy bar styles so it wins.
standouts: The explicit decision to center the bar variant via auto margins instead of translateX to avoid fighting FLIP animations.
The inline comment claiming this file is intentionally loaded after legacy bar styles to override them.
Gradient and shadow colors have inline fallback values rather than relying solely on CSS variables.
Starting-point chips are visually tied to the hero state but styled as a separate block.
conventions: BEM-style class naming with double underscores and double dashes.
CSS custom properties for colors, radii, durations, and easing.
Shared base component with modifier-only overrides for scale and positioning.
Circular icon buttons sized in multiples of 2px.
Transitions declared on transform, background, border-color, and color for interactive feedback.
concerns: The fixed bar width uses calc(100% - 480px), which may be too narrow or cause overflow on smaller viewports; no media queries are present.
z-index: 30 is declared without any documented stacking context strategy.
backdrop-filter can cause accessibility and performance issues on some devices.
No visible focus styles for .composer__btn beyond hover states.
No reduced-motion or dark-mode considerations are visible in this file.
.composer__btn--ghost only has a hover rule; there is no base .composer__btn--ghost style.
notes: A polished, animation-aware CSS module for a chat/query composer that reuses one base structure across welcome, floating studio, and inline interview contexts.
questions: What is the 'Vi mark' and what icon/content is intended for .composer__mark?
What are the exact values of the custom properties such as --accent-gold, --ink, and --dur-slow?
Which legacy bar styles does this file override, and how is load order enforced?
How are the starting-point chips wired to populate or focus the input?
Is there accompanying JavaScript that performs the shared-element FLIP animation mentioned in the comments?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: one shared input surface with three state variants that adjust scale and positioning.
apparent_themes: Warm, premium tactile UI with gold accents
Glassmorphism in the floating bar variant
Progressive enhancement via CSS variable fallbacks
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to unify the composer experience across multiple product surfaces while leaving transforms untouched so that a shared-element animation can move the composer between contexts smoothly.
novelty: The deliberate avoidance of a translateX centering transform on the fixed bar to reserve transform for FLIP-based shared-element animation is a file-local design signal.
contribution: Provides a reusable, context-variant composer stylesheet with consistent visual language, interaction states, and an animation-safe centering strategy that a fused system could keep for any surface needing a branded input bar.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet defining a flex-based composer component with hero, fixed bar, and inline variants, including an input, action buttons, a leading mark, and starting-point suggestion chips.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, context-adaptive styled input surface for a chat or query composer that supports shared-element transitions and consistent branding.
